news.title: SK Hynix plans to double wafer capacity in next five years, group chairman says
news.summary: The chairman of South Korea's SK Group said on Tuesday its memory chip ‌unit SK Hynix aims to double wafer capacity ‌over the next five years.  Chey Tae-won was speaking at the Computex conference ​in Taipei, where executives from some of the world's top technology companies, including Nvidia, are gathering.  He added that he hopes his company can be a major high-bandwidth memory (HBM) supplier for Nvidia's Vera Rubin system.

news.title: Exxon Mobil Weighs Venezuela Return And Valuation Upside For Investors
news.summary: Exxon Mobil is reportedly in talks to secure new oil production rights in Venezuela after years away from the country. The move focuses on renewed access to some of the world's largest oil reserves and could reshape the company's global asset base. news.title: TotalEnergies Expands Renewables Portfolio As Oil Market Faces New Disruptions
news.summary: TotalEnergies has filed for government approval to build a 1.5 GW offshore wind farm off the coast of Normandy, which would be France's largest renewables project. The company has also agreed a $2.2b renewables joint venture with Masdar, combining assets across nine Asian countries. Both moves come as global energy markets face disruption from a geopolitical crisis affecting oil supply. news.title: Tesla Faces China FSD Lawsuit As Robotics Competition Tests Valuation
news.summary: Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) is facing a new class-action lawsuit in China alleging false advertising and consumer fraud related to its Full Self-Driving feature.
